infuse 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • infuse: A Quick Start Guide

    Harvest Win/Loss Influence-Based Results

    harvest.Rd

    The primary engine for gathering analytic results from a sow object. This function passes weighted ECDF data to the C++ inference engine to calculate point estimates and influence functions for Wins, Losses, Net Benefit, and Win Ratios.

    harvest(fit, lambda = 0.5)

    Arguments

    fit

    An S3 object of class "sow" generated by the sow() function.

    lambda

    A numeric vector of thresholds defining the margin of clinical importance for each outcome. Recycled if shorter than the number of outcomes.

    Value

    A list containing three data frames:

    • theta: Point estimates (Favorable, Unfavorable, Net Benefit, Win Ratio) for each outcome.

    • IF_x: Influence function values and metadata for the Treated group.

    • IF_y: Influence function values and metadata for the Control group.

    Details

    The function calculates:

    • Favorable (f): Probability the treated group wins.

    • Unfavorable (u): Probability the treated group loses.

    • Net Benefit (n): \(f - u\).

    • Win Ratio (w): \(\log(f) - \log(u)\) (modeled in log-space for influence).

    Examples

    # ---------------------------------------------------------
# Example 1: Survival Data (Ipilimumab)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
data(ipilimumab)
# Higher time is "good" for Progression-Free Survival
fit <- sow(Surv(time, event) ~ arm,
                 data = ipilimumab,
                 treated = "ipilimumab",
                 good = "high")

h <- harvest(fit,lambda = 6)
#> Error in mutate(IF_x, u = ooo2$IF_x[order(orx)]): could not find function "mutate"
print(h)
#> Error: object 'h' not found
summary(h)
#> Error: object 'h' not found
plot(h,metric="netb",type = "estimate")
#> Error: object 'h' not found

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Example 2: Continuous Data (Obstetrics)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
data(obstetrics)
# High birthweight is generally considered "good"
fit <- sow(Birthweight ~ Group,
                data = obstetrics,
                treated = "T",
                good = "high")

h <- harvest(fit,lambda = 500)
#> Error in mutate(IF_x, u = ooo2$IF_x[order(orx)]): could not find function "mutate"
print(h)
#> Error: object 'h' not found
summary(h)
#> Error: object 'h' not found
plot(h,metric="wr",type = "influence")
#> Error: object 'h' not found

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Example 3: Longitudinal Binary Data (Respiratory)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
data(respiratory)
# We analyze status at the final visit (Visit 4)
resp_v4 <- respiratory[respiratory$visit == 4, ]
sown_resp <- sow(outcome ~ treat,
                 data = resp_v4,
                 treated = "A",
                 good = "high")

h <- harvest(fit,lambda = 0.5)
#> Error in mutate(IF_x, u = ooo2$IF_x[order(orx)]): could not find function "mutate"
print(h)
#> Error: object 'h' not found
summary(h)
#> Error: object 'h' not found
plot(h,metric="wr",type = "influence")
#> Error: object 'h' not found

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Example 4: Multiple Outcomes (Epilepsy)
# ---------------------------------------------------------
data(epilepsy)
# Looking at seizure rate at Period 4. Lower rate is "good".
epi_v4 <- epilepsy[epilepsy$period == 4, ]
sown_epi <- sow(seizure.rate ~ treatment,
                data = epi_v4,
                treated = "Progabide",
                good = "low")

h <- harvest(fit,lambda = 15)
#> Error in mutate(IF_x, u = ooo2$IF_x[order(orx)]): could not find function "mutate"
print(h)
#> Error: object 'h' not found
summary(h)
#> Error: object 'h' not found
plot(h,metric="wr",type = "influence")
#> Error: object 'h' not found
